title: lupinus pusillus var. intermontanus
intro: Lupinus pusillus var. intermontanus, also known as the Sierra lupine or intermountain lupine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family. It is native to the western United States and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Lupinus pusillus var. intermontanus can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal benefits.
